prediction.reasoning: Predicting stock prices over a short time frame such as one day is highly uncertain and subject to various market factors. In this example, I have assumed a slight upward movement in the point estimate based on an average daily volatility of around 1% typically seen in stocks like NVDA. The confidence intervals represent a range of potential prices that encompass this inherent volatility, with wider intervals indicating more uncertainty. These intervals are purely illustrative and do not guarantee real-market outcomes.
